item.title: BETSY JOHNSON, AN ODE TO TRACEY EMIN, TO ACCUMULATION Autre is proud to announce...
item.description:
@autremagazine

BETSY JOHNSON, AN ODE TO TRACEY EMIN, TO ACCUMULATION

Autre is proud to announce its FW25 WORK IN PROGRESS Issue (preorder link in bio). We are living through a moment of constant upheaval—where change is relentless, and nothing feels fixed. This issue embraces that reality, exploring radical ideas and new visions, from the office to the edges of the universe. Cultural multihyphenate @betsyjohnson_, photographed by @pavelgolik, in a deep dive conversation with Martina Tiefenthaler (@mrt4000).

Hailing from Grimsby, Betsy Johnson is a stylist, creative director, and artist whose eclectic practice blends punk and glamour, minimalism and maximalism, into a singular visual language. For the Work In Progress theme, she created an homage to Tracey Emin’s My Bed using objects from her own home and studio. In parallel, Austrian-born Martina Tiefenthaler—former Chief Creative Officer at Balenciag—brings her architectural precision and recent explorations in coaching and image making to the dialogue. Together, Johnson and Tiefenthaler reflect on the emotional charge of objects, balancing intuition against commercial demands, and the need for more human-centered approaches to leadership in creative fields.

Preorder the issue now by clicking the 🔗 in bio (www.autre.love).

All preorders come with a FULL
digital preview delivered to your inbox immediately after checkout while you wait for the print edition
to arrive.
photography PAVEL GOLIK
set design @samuelfasse
art direction ELIAS @ B27
#betsyjohnson #traceyemin #pavelgolik #martinatiefenthaler #fashionindustry

@autremagazine

BETSY JOHNSON, AN ODE TO TRACEY EMIN, TO ACCUMULATION

Autre is proud to announce its FW25 WORK IN PROGRESS Issue (preorder link in bio). We are living through a moment of constant upheaval—where change is relentless, and nothing feels fixed. This issue embraces that reality, exploring radical ideas and new visions, from the office to the edges of the universe. Cultural multihyphenate @betsyjohnson_, photographed by @pavelgolik, in a deep dive conversation with Martina Tiefenthaler (@mrt4000).

Hailing from Grimsby, Betsy Johnson is a stylist, creative director, and artist whose eclectic practice blends punk and glamour, minimalism and maximalism, into a singular visual language. For the Work In Progress theme, she created an homage to Tracey Emin’s My Bed using objects from her own home and studio. In parallel, Austrian-born Martina Tiefenthaler—former Chief Creative Officer at Balenciag—brings her architectural precision and recent explorations in coaching and image making to the dialogue. Together, Johnson and Tiefenthaler reflect on the emotional charge of objects, balancing intuition against commercial demands, and the need for more human-centered approaches to leadership in creative fields.

Preorder the issue now by clicking the 🔗 in bio (www.autre.love).

All preorders come with a FULL
digital preview delivered to your inbox immediately after checkout while you wait for the print edition
to arrive.
photography PAVEL GOLIK
set design @samuelfasse
art direction ELIAS @ B27
#betsyjohnson #traceyemin #pavelgolik #martinatiefenthaler #fashionindustry
item.author: autremagazine

item.title: LUCIEN PAGÈS FOR AUTRE WIP Autre is proud to announce its FW25 WORK IN PROGRESS ...
item.description:
@autremagazine

LUCIEN PAGÈS FOR AUTRE WIP

Autre is proud to announce its FW25 WORK IN PROGRESS Issue (preorder link in bio). We are living through a moment of constant upheaval—where change is relentless, and nothing feels fixed. This issue embraces that reality, exploring radical ideas and new visions, from the office to the edges of the universe. As we enter a critical fashion season with majorly anticipated designer debuts, nobody has seen the pendulum swing more than @lucienpages .

Over the past twenty years, Lucien Pagès has managed to have his cake and eat it too. In a system of extremes: spectacle and collapse, hype and fatigue, visibility and disappearance, Pagès has made a career out of mastering fluctuations. Since founding his agency in 2006, he has represented more than 150 brands while keeping his own presence understated, building influence not through noise but through careful calibration, crisis management, and an instinct for the shifting rhythms of the industry. In conversation with @ly.as, Pagès delivers a shapeshifting interview — equal parts candid honesty and finely tuned media training.

Preorder the issue now by clicking the 🔗 in bio (www.autre.love). All preorders come with a FULL digital preview delivered to your inbox immediately after checkout while you wait for the print edition to arrive.

Photography @jesperdlund

Art Director @martinmajor
Type @abcdinamo


#lucienpages #fashionweek #paris #lyas
item.content:encoded:
@autremagazine

LUCIEN PAGÈS FOR AUTRE WIP

Autre is proud to announce its FW25 WORK IN PROGRESS Issue (preorder link in bio). We are living through a moment of constant upheaval—where change is relentless, and nothing feels fixed. This issue embraces that reality, exploring radical ideas and new visions, from the office to the edges of the universe. As we enter a critical fashion season with majorly anticipated designer debuts, nobody has seen the pendulum swing more than @lucienpages .

Over the past twenty years, Lucien Pagès has managed to have his cake and eat it too. In a system of extremes: spectacle and collapse, hype and fatigue, visibility and disappearance, Pagès has made a career out of mastering fluctuations. Since founding his agency in 2006, he has represented more than 150 brands while keeping his own presence understated, building influence not through noise but through careful calibration, crisis management, and an instinct for the shifting rhythms of the industry. In conversation with @ly.as, Pagès delivers a shapeshifting interview — equal parts candid honesty and finely tuned media training.

Preorder the issue now by clicking the 🔗 in bio (www.autre.love). All preorders come with a FULL digital preview delivered to your inbox immediately after checkout while you wait for the print edition to arrive.

Photography @jesperdlund

Art Director @martinmajor
Type @abcdinamo


#lucienpages #fashionweek #paris #lyas
item.author: autremagazine

item.title: MAYA HAWKE, THERE’S NO BUSINESS LIKE… Autre is proud to announce its FW25 WORK I...
item.description:
@autremagazine

MAYA HAWKE, THERE’S NO BUSINESS LIKE…

Autre is proud to announce its FW25 WORK IN PROGRESS Issue (preorder link in bio). We are living through a moment of constant upheaval—where change is relentless, and nothing feels fixed. This issue embraces that reality, exploring radical ideas and new visions, from the office to the edges of the universe. Our second cover features star of stage and screen Maya Hawke in conversation with fellow actor @lilyrabe All clothing @prada FW25

From Stranger Things to Tarantino to Off-Broadway, Maya Hawke has proven herself as one of the most versatile voices of her generation. Now, with a new role in The Hunger Games and the final season of Stranger Things, she joins Lily Rabe to talk process and vulnerability.

Preorder the issue now by clicking the 🔗 in bio (www.autre.love). All preorders come with a FULL
digital preview delivered to your inbox immediately after checkout while you wait for the print edition to arrive.

Photographer Boe Marion @boe_marion
Lead Stylist Cece Liu @cc_looo
Consulting Stylist Jamie Mizrahi @sweetbabyjamie
Hair Peter Butler @peterbutlerhair
Makeup Benjamin Puckey @benjaminpuckey
Director of Photography Basil Fauchier @basilfauchier
First Assistant Camera Arjun Bajaj @arjunbajaja
Gaffer Mario Riquelme @mario_nomas
Photo Assistant Martin Axell @martinaxell
Production Family Projects @family.projects
Production Assistant Myles Gouveia @mylesgouveia

Art Director @martinmajor
Type @abcdinamo

#mayahawke #strangerthings #hungergames #workinprogress #wip #pradafw25
#pradaeditorials

@autremagazine

MAYA HAWKE, THERE’S NO BUSINESS LIKE…

Autre is proud to announce its FW25 WORK IN PROGRESS Issue (preorder link in bio). We are living through a moment of constant upheaval—where change is relentless, and nothing feels fixed. This issue embraces that reality, exploring radical ideas and new visions, from the office to the edges of the universe. Our second cover features star of stage and screen Maya Hawke in conversation with fellow actor @lilyrabe All clothing @prada FW25

From Stranger Things to Tarantino to Off-Broadway, Maya Hawke has proven herself as one of the most versatile voices of her generation. Now, with a new role in The Hunger Games and the final season of Stranger Things, she joins Lily Rabe to talk process and vulnerability.

Preorder the issue now by clicking the 🔗 in bio (www.autre.love). All preorders come with a FULL
digital preview delivered to your inbox immediately after checkout while you wait for the print edition to arrive.
